summary: Comprehensive testing of Turismo que se Sente (accessible Natal tourism guide). Backend: 14/14 pytest cases PASSED covering all spots CRUD, filters, categories, seed idempotency, and confirmation that _id is never leaked. Frontend (Expo Web mobile viewport 390x844): Home page renders welcome, search, quick-access grid (6), featured carousel (6), and all 14 spots; tapping a featured card navigates to /spot/{id} which shows hero image, badges (Acessível/Banheiro acessível/Piso tátil), full description, 'Ouvir audiodescrição' CTA and accessibility list; /audio/{id} loads with title, neighborhood, image, transcript and player UI; /near loads with category chips and 'Praia' filter returns 7 spots correctly; /menu page renders profile/accessibility/settings sections. Admin: list shows 14 spots with edit/delete; CREATE works (backend confirmed), EDIT works (name updated and reflected in list). DELETE from admin on web preview did NOT actually delete the record because React-Native Web's Alert.alert does not render a confirmation dialog with callbacks (the destructive onPress never fires on web). On real Expo Go / native mobile this would work, but on web preview this flow is broken. Search filter on home works (entering 'Ponta' filters to 3 items). No red-screen errors, 0 page errors. Only RN-Web deprecation warnings observed.

ui_bugs.issue: On Expo Web preview the Alert.alert confirmation dialog used for delete never shows buttons / never invokes the destructive callback (RN-Web limitation). DELETE button thus appears to do nothing on web. Native mobile is unaffected.

issues: Uses deprecated shadowColor/shadowOpacity/shadowRadius style props; RN-Web 0.79+ recommends boxShadow. Generates console warnings but no functional impact.

react_native_issues.issue: React Native Alert with confirm-style buttons is not fully supported on react-native-web; the destructive button's onPress never fires on web. Consider a custom Modal-based confirm for cross-platform support. Reference: /app/frontend/app/admin.tsx:78-94
Deprecated 'shadow*' style props in use; should migrate to boxShadow for RN-Web compatibility. Reference: /app/frontend/app/near.tsx:210-213

action_items: Replace Alert.alert delete confirmation in /app/frontend/app/admin.tsx with a cross-platform Modal (or Platform.select to use window.confirm on web) so admin DELETE works in web preview as well as native.
Migrate deprecated shadow* style props in /app/frontend/app/near.tsx (updateBtn) to boxShadow per RN-Web 0.79+ guidelines (low priority).
critical_code_review_comments: Backend correctly excludes _id via projection {"_id": 0} in all reads — verified.
Backend uses async/await consistently and HTTPException with proper codes (404, 400).
Frontend api.ts correctly reads process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL with no hardcoded fallback.
All interactive elements have testID attributes — good test coverage support.
Admin save handler has no loading indicator during API call (minor UX gap).
POST /api/spots returns 200 instead of conventional 201 Created (minor REST style).

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all flows work; admin delete fails only on web due to RN-Web Alert.alert limitation, native mobile unaffected)

seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 14 Natal tourist spots on startup; /api/seed endpoint reseeds idempotently. Tests reseed at module start and clean up created TEST_ spots.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend stable. Re-test admin DELETE only if main agent changes confirmation flow. TTS (expo-speech) on web may silently fail because browsers often lack pt-BR voices — test on Expo Go / device for full TTS verification.
rca_of_issue: Admin DELETE on web: React-Native-Web's Alert API does not render the multi-button confirm dialog with destructive callbacks. The Alert.alert call in admin.tsx (lines 78-94) opens a console-only stub and the destructive onPress never executes, so no DELETE /api/spots/{id} request is fired. Mitigation: implement a custom <Modal> confirm or use Platform.OS === 'web' ? window.confirm(...) : Alert.alert(...).
